chat.title: Chat
chat.description: Ask about your inbox, or tell the agent to draft, label, or clean up mail.

home.title: Home
home.description: What needs you, what the agent prepared, and what ran recently.

leads.title: Leads
leads.description: Every prospect the agent is tracking, from first inquiry to closed.

automations.title: Automations
automations.description: Run the agent on a schedule with a standing instruction.

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Documents and memory for your agent.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Your AI, connected accounts, permissions, and preferences.

errors.network: Can't reach the server. Check that it's running and try again.
errors.timeout: The server took too long to respond. Please try again.
errors.unavailable: The server is unavailable right now. Please try again shortly.
errors.server: Something went wrong on the server. Please try again.
errors.notFound: That couldn't be found. It may have been removed.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.request: That didn't work. Please try again.

chat.emptyBody: The agent can search, label, and draft across your connected accounts.
chat.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently deletes the conversation and its messages.

draft.sendConfirm: Send this draft now? It goes out exactly as written.
draft.discardConfirm: Discard this draft? It is removed from your mail account.

messageDraft.sendConfirm: Send this message now? It goes out exactly as written.

briefing.label: Briefing
briefing.draftReplyPrompt: Draft a reply to {{sender}}'s email "{{subject}}" in {{account}} (thread {{threadId}}). The briefing summarized it as: {{gist}}
briefing.draftReplyPromptNoAccount: Draft a reply to {{sender}}'s email "{{subject}}" (thread {{threadId}}). The briefing summarized it as: {{gist}}
briefing.reviewDraftPrompt: Show me the draft you wrote for {{sender}}'s email "{{subject}}" (thread {{threadId}}, draft {{draftId}}) so I can review it.
briefing.askAboutPrompt: In this morning's briefing you flagged {{sender}}'s email "{{subject}}" (thread {{threadId}}), "{{gist}}". What is this about, and does anything need me?

showcase.intro: Here is a sample of everything I can show in chat, tool activity, one of every card, a formatted reply, and the thinking state.
showcase.toolsNote: While tools run, each one appears as a chip: finished, still running, or failed.

search.title: Search
search.placeholder: Search chats, briefings, drafts, documents, memories…
search.hint: Search across your chats, briefings, drafts, documents and memories.
search.noResultsHint: Check the spelling, or try a shorter word.

whatsapp.pairIntro: Scan the QR code with your phone: WhatsApp → Settings → Linked devices → Link a device. Your chats are mirrored locally on this computer and never leave it.
whatsapp.pairQrHint: The code refreshes automatically while pairing is open.
whatsapp.pairExpired: The QR code expired before it was scanned.
whatsapp.unlinkConfirm: Unlink this device and delete the locally mirrored chats? Your WhatsApp account and the chats on your phone are not affected.

onoffice.setupIntro: Create an API user in onOffice, generate an access token, and paste the token and secret below. They stay on this computer and are never included in a backup.
onoffice.keepPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved value
onoffice.removeSavedConfirm: Remove the saved onOffice credentials from this computer? The assistant will lose access to your CRM until you add them again.

connections.customToggleOn: Email runs through the Pipedream project configured below.
connections.builtinInUse: Email runs through Trailin's built-in Pipedream.
connections.builtinMissing: This build has no built-in Pipedream, turn this on and add your own below.
connections.searchProviders: Search all apps… (Gmail, Zoho, IMAP, Notion, …)
connections.anyAppHint: These are just a few, search above to connect any of 2,000+ apps, from other mail services to your calendar, notes and chat.
connections.setupTitle: One-time setup: connect Trailin to Pipedream
connections.setupIntro: Trailin uses <pd>Pipedream</pd>, a free service, to link your email accounts safely, as many as you like, also several from the same provider. It needs three values from your own Pipedream workspace, copied once:
connections.setupStep2: Under Settings → API, create an OAuth client and copy its Client ID and Client Secret below.
connections.setupStep3: Create a project (any name) and paste its URL, or the proj_… id, below.
connections.removeSavedConfirm: Remove the Pipedream credentials saved in the app?

permissions.hint: Reading is always allowed, the assistant can read and search this account. Everything else needs its own grant:
permissions.hintEmail: Reading is always allowed, the assistant can read and search this account and prepare drafts. Everything else needs its own grant:

write.title: Create & change
write.on: May create and change things in this account when you ask it to.
write.off: Cannot create or change anything in this account.
write.offEmail: Cannot change this mailbox, creating drafts is always allowed.
write.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to create & change as {{account}}?
write.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can create and change things in this account when you ask it to. Sending and deleting stay separate switches. You can turn this off anytime.

send.title: Send
send.on: May send messages from this account when you ask it to.
send.off: Cannot send anything from this account.
send.offEmail: Cannot send, outgoing email stays as drafts for your review.
send.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to send as {{account}}?
send.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can send messages as {{account}} when you ask it to. You can turn this off anytime.

delete.title: Delete
delete.on: May delete things in this account when you ask it to.
delete.off: Cannot delete anything in this account.
delete.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to delete as {{account}}?
delete.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can delete things in this account when you ask it to. You can turn this off anytime.

onoffice.title: Automations may create records
onoffice.rowOn: Automations may create CRM records (contacts, appointments, tasks, links), never edit, delete or send.
onoffice.rowOff: Automations only read the CRM; records are created in chat, with your confirmation.
onoffice.confirmBody: Scheduled runs can then create contacts, appointments, tasks and links in your live CRM on their own, your colleagues will see those records. Editing, deleting and sending stay chat-only. You can turn this off anytime.

onofficeWrites.title: Edit, delete & send
onofficeWrites.rowOn: May modify or delete CRM records and send through the CRM when you ask it to in chat.
onofficeWrites.rowOff: Chat can read the CRM and create records; editing, deleting and sending stay off until you arm this.
onofficeWrites.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to edit, delete and send in onOffice?
onofficeWrites.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can modify and delete live CRM records and send through onOffice when you ask it to in chat. Scheduled runs never get these tools. You can turn this off anytime.

whatsappSend.title: Auto-send
whatsappSend.rowOn: WhatsApp drafts are sent automatically, including from automations that explicitly send.
whatsappSend.rowOff: WhatsApp messages are saved as drafts and sent only after you approve them.
whatsappSend.confirmBody: Once on, WhatsApp messages are sent immediately whenever an instruction or automation explicitly asks to send, with no draft to review. Without this, every message stays a draft with a Send button. You can turn this off anytime.

connections.noAccounts: No email accounts connected yet. Use the buttons above, you can connect several accounts per provider.
connections.finishConnecting: Complete the connection in the browser tab that just opened, the account appears here automatically once it's linked.
connections.learnVoiceStarted: Learning your writing style from {{name}}, it'll appear in Settings shortly.

home.setupBanner: Trailin isn't fully set up, the agent can't reach your mail yet.
home.offlineBanner: Can't reach your email provider right now, check your connection.
home.missedBanner_one: 1 automation didn't run while the app was closed.
home.missedBanner_other: {{count}} automations didn't run while the app was closed.
home.triggerCatchUpDetail: Catch-up run for the slot due {{when}}, covering everything since the previous run.
home.triggerTodoDetail: Started when you completed the to-do "{{title}}".
home.attentionEmpty: All clear. New tasks and approvals show up here.
home.activityEmptyBody: Put your inbox on autopilot: a standing instruction runs on a schedule and reports here.
home.activityNoRunsBody: Your automations haven't produced results yet, run one now or wait for its schedule.

drafts.discardConfirm: Discard this draft? It is removed from your mail account.
drafts.sendConfirm: Send this draft now? It goes out exactly as written.
drafts.subject: Subject
drafts.refineInChat: Refine in the chat that wrote it

threadHistory.empty: No earlier messages in this conversation.

automations.formHint: Give the agent a standing instruction and choose when it runs.
automations.name: Name
automations.dateOnceHint: Runs once on this date, then turns off automatically.
automations.manualHint: Never runs on its own, only when you press “Run now”.
automations.advancedLossNote: The custom cron expression was replaced by the schedule above.
automations.cronInvalid: Doesn’t look like a standard 5-field cron, checked on create.
automations.instructionPlaceholder: Summarize all unread emails from the last 24 hours…
automations.showInActivityHint: Include this automation's runs in the Home activity feed.
automations.runOnNewMail: Run immediately when new mail arrives
automations.runOnNewMailHint: Also runs immediately when new inbound mail is detected, in addition to the schedule.
automations.notifyOnCompletionHint: Shows a desktop notification when a run completes.
automations.emptyBody: Write a standing instruction and choose when it runs.

suggestions.title: Suggested automations
suggestions.hint: Patterns from your recent chats, accept one to schedule it, or dismiss it and it won't come back.

leads.formHint: Record a prospect by hand, email inquiries are filed automatically.
leads.name: Name
leads.mergedNote: {{email}} was already recorded, the details were merged.
leads.emptyBody: When an email shows someone interested, the agent records them here, or add one yourself.
leads.deleteConfirm: Delete lead “{{email}}”? Automations attached to this lead are deleted too.

library.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{title}}"? The file is also removed from the folder.
library.deleteFolderConfirm: The folder "{{title}}" and every file inside it will be deleted.
library.deleteManyConfirm_one: {{count}} entry will be deleted, folders with everything inside them.
library.deleteManyConfirm_other: {{count}} entries will be deleted, folders with everything inside them.

setup.title: Welcome to Trailin
setup.intro: Your mail, handled by an agent you can talk to. Two things and you're in:
setup.stepAiDescription: Use a subscription you already have (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT) or paste an API key. Your sign-in stays on this computer.
setup.stepEmailDescription: Link Gmail or Outlook so the agent can see your mail. You can add more accounts later.
setup.pipedreamMissingBody: This build has no built-in email bridge, Trailin connects through your own free Pipedream project instead. Set it up once right here, then link your accounts:
setup.advancedLink: Advanced: use my own Pipedream project
setup.allSetBody: The agent can now read your mail, draft replies, and answer your questions.
setup.allSetReadOnly: Trailin starts read-only: the agent drafts and answers, but never sends, changes or deletes anything until you allow it per account, the shield on each account's row in Settings.
setup.skip: Skip for now, I'll look around first
setup.offlineBody: Checking connection to Trailin's server every few seconds, this will pick up automatically once it's back.

ai.title: AI & model
ai.description: The AI that reads and answers your mail. Sign in with a subscription you already have (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT) or use an API key, your sign-in stays on this computer.

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.description: Email, CRM, and messaging accounts, and what each one is allowed to do.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: How Trailin looks, speaks, and tells time.

data.title: Local data
data.description: Everything Trailin has stored on this computer, and how to back it up.

about.title: About
about.description: The version you're running, and updates when a newer one ships.

language.title: Language
language.description: Used for the app and for the assistant's answers, in chat and in automations.

quickActions.title: Quick actions
quickActions.description: What buttons like "Draft reply" do: send the request right away, or put it in the chat box so you can edit it first.

appearance.label: Appearance
appearance.description: Light, dark, or follow this device.

timezone.label: Timezone
timezone.description: Automations run and times are shown in this timezone

backup.label: Backup
backup.description: A snapshot of the local database, chats, drafts, memories, and automations, but not your connected accounts' credentials.

about.tagline: Local-first AI assistant for your email and leads
about.unavailable: Update checks aren't available in this build

settings.signInFirst: Sign in to a provider above, then choose its model here.
settings.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste API key for {{provider}}, then press Enter…
settings.loginInputPlaceholder: Paste the code / redirect URL if it doesn't finish…

fileAccess.title: File access
fileAccess.description: What the assistant may do with files outside its own Trailin folder. That folder (memory, skills, your document library) is always available to it.

read.title: Read files
read.on: May list folders, find files, search their contents (grep), and read files when you ask it to.
read.off: Can only read files in its own Trailin folder.
read.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to read files?
read.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can list folders, find files, search their contents (grep), and read files anywhere on this computer when you ask it to, your Downloads, Documents, and everything else your account can see. Instructions hidden in an email could try to make it read private files, so it is told never to pass file contents on without your say-so. You can turn this off anytime.

write.title: Write files
write.on: May create new files (write) and edit existing ones (edit) when you ask it to.
write.off: Can only create and change files in its own Trailin folder.
write.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to write files?
write.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can create new files (write) and edit existing ones (edit) anywhere on this computer when you ask it to. Instructions hidden in an email could try to trigger unwanted changes. You can turn this off anytime.

bash.title: Run commands
bash.on: May run shell commands as you (bash) when you ask it to.
bash.confirmTitle: Allow the AI to run commands?
bash.confirmBody: Once on, the assistant can run real shell commands as you, with everything your user account can do. Instructions hidden in an email you receive could try to trigger harmful ones. Only arm this if you understand that risk. You can turn it off anytime.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: This screen ran into a rendering error. You can try again, or reload the app.
